message:
fix(fcp-auth-schema): preserve out-of-range integers in SchemaError diagnostics

Follow-up to the fresh-eyes audit (c16dcb2c). While reviewing the
claim-decoding paths, two related diagnostic gaps turned up:

1. Out-of-range integer conversions were collapsing into
   `UnexpectedType { got: "out-of-range integer" }`, which hid the
   actual offending value. That type is reserved for CBOR
   type-mismatches (e.g. Text where Integer was expected) and is
   the wrong shape for "right type, wrong magnitude" failures.

2. The `timestamp()` helper's fallback path reported `value: 0` in
   `InvalidTimestamp` when the i128 -> i64 narrowing failed, which
   discarded the original value entirely and produced misleading
   error logs.

## Change

Added a new `SchemaError::OutOfRange { label, value: i128, expected }`
variant that preserves the full CBOR integer range and names the
destination type. Routed three call sites onto it:

- `expect_u64` — now returns OutOfRange{expected: "u64"} with the
  observed i128 when `u64::try_from` fails (previously UnexpectedType).
- `expect_u16` — same, with expected: "u16".
- `timestamp` — when the seconds value doesn't fit i64, returns
  OutOfRange{expected: "i64 timestamp"} with the actual i128 instead
  of a bogus `InvalidTimestamp{value: 0}`.

`InvalidTimestamp` is preserved for the narrower "fit i64 but chrono
rejects it" path (e.g., timestamp beyond chrono's year 262143 ceiling),
and its docstring is tightened to say exactly that.

## Tests

Three regression tests added under the fresh-eyes audit module:

- `decode_out_of_range_u16_yields_out_of_range_error_with_value` —
  feeds 100_000 into a u16 slot and asserts the full value round-trips.
- `decode_out_of_range_u64_preserves_value` — feeds -1 into a u64 slot
  and asserts the negative value survives through to the error.
- `decode_invalid_timestamp_reports_actual_value_not_zero` — feeds
  i64::MAX as an EXP timestamp and asserts that InvalidTimestamp
  reports the actual seconds (not 0) — the exact regression the
  earlier drafts had.

No behavior change on the happy path; only error shape / contents.
